old_claim.note: Basic Weaving and Basketry is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: Excavated bins, pits, and silos are fixed storage features and do not require woven basket manufacture as their historical lineage.

source_shape.source_locator: The excavation describes storage bins, silos, and raised-floor granaries through their structural remains; it does not identify woven containers as a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source documents fixed PPNA storage structures rather than a basketry-dependent container technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scope now follows excavated fixed features, so a separate portable weaving tradition adds no defensible causal relationship.

ontology_before: Food-storage pits carried a generic historical edge from basketry.
ontology_after: Fixed bins and pits stand as an independently evidenced storage practice.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: food_storage_pits no longer directly depends on basic_weaving_basketry.
would_reject_if: Direct excavation evidence shows the scoped bins or pits were constructed from woven basket forms.
short_reason: Fixed excavated storage structures do not require a basketry prerequisite.
